components.title: Fibre Channel Switch
components.description: Fibre Channel fabric switch providing zoned, high-speed connectivity between virtualisation hosts and SAN storage arrays. Deployed in a redundant dual-fabric (A/B) configuration so that a single switch or path failure does not interrupt storage access. Zoning is configured at the WWN level to enforce least-privilege storage access between initiators and targets.
components.purpose: Provide resilient, zoned Fibre Channel fabric connectivity between compute and storage, ensuring hosts can only see the storage volumes they are explicitly zoned to access.

props.name: fabric-topology
props.value: Dual redundant fabric (Fabric A / Fabric B), no single point of failure

props.name: speed
props.value: 32 Gbps Fibre Channel (backward compatible to 16/8 Gbps)

protocols.name: ssh
protocols.title: SSH — CLI management and fabric zoning configuration

protocols.name: snmp
protocols.title: SNMP — Fabric health and port utilisation monitoring

protocols.name: syslog
protocols.title: Syslog — Fabric event and audit log forwarding to SIEM

protocols.name: ntp
protocols.title: NTP — Time synchronisation across the fabric

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for the Fibre Channel Switch.

implemented-requirements.description: An approved hardening configuration is maintained for Fibre Channel switches, covering: disabling unused management protocols, restricting management access to the dedicated out-of-band VLAN, enabling role-based administrator accounts, and keeping switch firmware current with vendor security releases.

Fabric zoning restricts each host initiator to only the storage target ports and LUNs it requires (single-initiator, single-target hard zoning by WWN), so a compromised or misconfigured host cannot see storage volumes belonging to unrelated systems.
Security-relevant fabric events (zoning changes, port state changes, unauthorised device login attempts, configuration changes) are centrally logged and forwarded to the SIEM via syslog.
Fabric administrators are assigned dedicated privileged accounts, separate from their standard user accounts, used solely for SAN fabric administration.
Multi-factor authentication is required for administrators authenticating to the Fibre Channel switch management interface, enforced via integration with the organisation's identity provider.

A change and configuration management plan governs zoning changes on the Fibre Channel fabric: all zoning changes are submitted as a change request, peer-reviewed before activation, and the active zoneset configuration is version-controlled and backed up before and after each change.
